What's your coffee order when you're at a café in Mexico — do you stick to familiar drinks or use ordering as a way to explore local specialties?
When you're buying clothing, how much does brand transparency (who made it, where, under what conditions) factor into your decision? Is there a price point where you stop caring as much?
Between online shopping and in-person browsing, what's your split? Do you research heavily online before buying in stores, or do you prefer one channel entirely?

What does your ideal 5-year roadmap look like? Are there specific milestones (financial, career, location, relationship) you actively plan toward, or do you prefer to stay flexible and let opportunities emerge?
When you meet new people in a city (at volleyball, coworking, etc.), how do you typically decide whether to invest in building a deeper friendship versus keeping it as a casual acquaintance?

What's your ideal temperature range when choosing a city? Are you someone who tolerates any heat as long as there's A/C, or do you have a hard upper limit where the heat starts affecting your mood or productivity?

When you're going out for drinks in Mexico — say Friday after volleyball — what's your default? Do you stick to beer, cocktails, or does it depend on the venue? Any go-to Mexican beers or cocktails you always order?

How's your Spanish coming along? Do you feel comfortable handling daily life (ordering food, Negotiating prices, doctor visits) in Spanish, or are there specific situations where you still need English or a translator?

What podcasts do you regularly listen to — if any? Are you into tech/AI podcasts, true crime, storytelling, or something else entirely? Do you mainly listen during commutes, workouts, or while doing chores?

Beyond volleyball, what other hobbies or creative outlets do you enjoy? Things like gaming, photography, music, woodworking, coding side projects — anything you do to unwind that isn't fitness or media consumption?

When you're learning Spanish, what specific scenarios feel most uncomfortable or where do you still get stuck? Is it more around formal vs. casual speech, understanding fast conversations, or expressing nuanced emotions/ideas?

When you're buying something — say a new gadget, clothes, or even food — how often do you actively choose products based on their environmental impact or company ethics? Is it a dealbreaker, a nice-to-have, or something you think about but don't let it override other factors like price or convenience?
What's your current fitness philosophy — are you mostly maintaining, trying to build specific things, or just showing up to feel good? Do you track metrics or go more by how you feel?
Do you have any regular meditation, journaling, or mindfulness practices? Or any spiritual/philosophical frameworks you lean on for perspective?

When things feel off — whether it's work stress, relationship friction, or just general burnout — what's your default way of processing it? Do you talk it through with people, work through it solo, seek professional help, or something else?

When you think about financial milestones — like hitting a savings target, reaching a certain investment balance, or being able to afford a specific lifestyle — do you keep those numbers private, share them with close friends/family, or track them somewhere visible as motivation?

Are there specific brands or products you actively seek out because of their sustainability practices — either because you love the brand or because you trust their ethics? Conversely, are there companies you avoid entirely based on environmental or labor concerns?

How do you feel about humidity? Is there a humidity level where a city goes from "fine" to "unbearable" for you, or does heat + humidity not bother you as long as there's good A/C and you can cool down at night?
What kind of reader are you — do you prefer physical books, Kindle, or audio? Are there specific genres or authors you keep coming back to, or do you read mostly non-fiction for self-improvement?
What's your relationship with gaming these days — console, PC, mobile, or no time for it? Do you prefer competitive games, chill exploration games, roguelikes, or something else entirely?

When you host friends at your place — whether for a dinner party, watching a game, or just hanging out — what's your hosting style? Do you go all out with prepared food and drinks, keep it casual with snacks and good music, or does it depend on who you're hosting?

What's your music setup like? Do you have a favorite speaker or headphones, do you host music listening sessions with friends, play an instrument, or just stream through your phone? What songs or artists have been on repeat lately?

When you're on vacation or taking time off, what's your ideal breakdown? Do you prefer an action-packed itinerary with activities every day, a mix of planned things and free time to wander, or do you mostly just want to relax, read, and not commit to anything?

What's your home aesthetic — are you minimalist with clean lines, do you love filling your space with plants and character, or do you have a specific style (mid-century, boho, industrial)? Any preferred furniture brands or places you shop for home items?

How do you handle gift-giving — do you prefer experiences over things, do you keep a running wishlist of things you actually want, or do you love finding creative personal gifts for others? Any budget range you consider standard for different occasions?
Do you own a car right now, or are you fully car-free? If you do have one, what's the make/model, and do you actually enjoy driving, or is it more of a necessary evil for getting around?

When a big life decision comes up — like whether to take a job, move to a new city, or make a major financial commitment — do you typically make a pros/cons list, talk it through with people you trust, trust your gut, or wait for more information? What's your decision-making process for the 'big stuff'?

What's your philosophy on spending money on yourself versus saving? Are you more of a 'you only live once' spender, a 'save aggressively and FI' type, or somewhere in between depending on the category?

Is there a category for how you approach self-care, grooming, and personal appearance beyond clothing? Things like skincare routines, how often you get haircuts, whether you care about personal care products, etc.

Do you have strong opinions about news and current events consumption? How do you stay informed — do you avoid the news entirely, have specific sources you trust, listen to podcasts, or just let algorithms show you what's happening?
What's your self-care and grooming routine like? Do you have specific products you swear by, how often do you get haircuts, and do you care about skincare or is it just the basics?

Where do you see your career heading in the next 3-5 years? Are you aiming for senior IC roles, considering management, thinking about starting something of your own, or is the 'remote software engineer' identity pretty stable for now?

When it comes to life planning, do you generally know what you want the next couple years to look like, or do you prefer to keep it loose and see where life takes you? Any specific things you DO plan (like visa timelines, visits home, big trips) versus things you leave open?

Outside of volleyball, what does your mindfulness or spiritual practice look like these days? Do you meditate, journal, pray, or lean on any particular philosophy or framework for perspective? Or is it more about movement and physical practices for you?

How do you handle the legal/immigration side of being a digital nomad? Do you actively research visa rules, work with an immigration lawyer, or just figure it out as you go — and what's your strategy for staying compliant while living in Mexico?

When you're cooking at home — are you someone who follows recipes closely, or do you improvise based on what you have? Do you have any signature dishes you've perfected, or do you prefer trying new things each time?

How do you handle random administrative tasks that pop up — things like renewing insurance, setting up utilities in a new city, dealing with taxes across two countries? Do you tackle them immediately, use a system to batch them, or procrastinate until they become urgent?

When you watch movies or TV — are you more of a 'watch whatever's popular' person, do you have specific genres you gravitate toward, or do you have a curated list of must-watch films? Do you prefer watching alone, with your partner, or is it a social activity with friends?

Do you have any strong political or civic engagement preferences — things like voting habits, causes you care about, whether you discuss politics with friends, or how you form opinions on societal issues?

When you're not working or doing your regular activities — like on a lazy Sunday or between tasks — what does that downtime look like? Do you scroll social media, work on side projects, read, nap, or something else entirely?

What's your current reading habit — are you reading anything right now, or do you go through phases where you read a lot and then don't pick up a book for months? Any books that have stuck with you lately?
When you play games, what are you actually playing these days? Are you working through a backlog, playing something specific with friends online, or just not finding time for it?

What's your approach to news and staying informed? Do you actively follow current events, avoid the news entirely, or have specific sources you check? How does consuming news (or not) affect your mood?

When it's not volleyball and not work, what does your ideal weekend or day off actually look like? Do you have a default way you like to spend free time, or does it totally depend on your mood and energy?
How do you think about your personal style beyond clothing — things like skincare, haircuts, grooming, or even how you present yourself in video calls? Do you have a routine, or is it pretty minimal?

What's your car situation right now — do you own one, or are you fully car-free depending on the city? If you do have a car, what's the make/model and do you actually enjoy driving, or is it more of a necessary utility?

When you think about your ideal friend group — not your current circle, but what you'd love — how many close friends feels 'right' to you? Are you someone who prefers a small tight-knit crew, or do you like having a bigger network of acquaintances?
What's your approach to learning new things outside of work? Are you someone who takes courses, reads books to learn, watches YouTube tutorials, or just dives in and figures it out as you go?

How do you think about risk and uncertainty in life? Are you someone who avoids risks whenever possible, or do you lean into calculated risks with upside? How does that show up in your career, finances, and where you choose to live?

What's your approach to healthcare and medical care while living abroad in Mexico? Do you have local health insurance, use public clinics, fly back to the US for serious stuff, or have a hybrid approach?
What podcasts do you actually listen to regularly — any specific shows, hosts, or genres you keep coming back to? Do you listen while working, during commutes, or only during dedicated downtime?

When you receive a gift, what's the ideal scenario for you — do you prefer practical things you can use, experiences over things, handwritten notes, or something else entirely? And do you keep a running wishlist somewhere, or do you just hope people know what you'd like?
What's your typical beverage routine beyond coffee — do you drink tea, alcohol, energy drinks, or just water? And when you're socializing at a bar or restaurant, what's your go-to order?

What's your approach to personal care and grooming — things like skincare, haircuts, beard grooming, fragrances, or even how you present yourself on video calls? Do you have specific products you use, or is it pretty minimal?

How do you handle making new friends or building community in a new city? Are you someone who actively puts yourself out there — joining classes, apps, asking people to hang out — or do you wait for connections to happen more organically?

When you buy things online — whether it's gear, clothes, or random stuff — which platforms do you actually use? Are you an Amazon-everything person, prefer buying direct from brands, or do you hunt for deals on specific sites?

What's your fitness 'why' right now? Are you training for something specific (a race, a sport, strength goals), primarily doing it for mental health and routine, or just maintaining so you can keep doing the activities you love like volleyball?

Are you actively learning any languages right now, or working toward a specific fluency goal? And when you meet someone who doesn't speak English, how do you handle communication — Google Translate, try to piece it together, or just default to English and see what happens?

What's your financial philosophy when it comes to investing? Are you index-fund-and-forget-it, more hands-on with individual stocks/crypto, or a bit of both? Do you have specific FIRE-style goals or just want enough to keep your options open?

What's your relationship with social media and online presence these days? Are you someone who posts regularly, only lurks, or mostly off-platform? And do you think about how you come across online, or is that not something you stress about?

When you buy things — whether it's a gadget, a piece of clothing, or random household stuff — how much does sustainability factor into your decision? Do you actively seek out ethical brands, don't really think about it, or is it somewhere in between depending on the category?

What's your skincare and grooming routine like beyond haircuts? Do you have a specific skincare regimen, any products you swear by, or is it pretty minimal? And how do you feel about fragrances or colognes — do you wear them regularly, save them for special occasions, or not really bother?

When you're at a bar or social gathering, what's your go-to drink order? Are you someone who sticks with one drink all night, likes trying new cocktails, or do you usually just grab a beer? How important is the 'vibe' of a bar or venue to you?

What's your relationship with alcohol and nightlife? Do you enjoy going out to bars, clubs, or late-night gatherings, or is your ideal night more about low-key hangouts? Any feelings about being the 'sober' one in a group, or do you usually match the energy of whoever you're with?

If you had unlimited resources and time, what are 2-3 skills or hobbies you would love to learn or get really good at? This could be anything — playing an instrument, woodworking, cooking a specific cuisine, learning a language to fluency, etc.
